perturbation.original_text: "Payment Date" means: (i) for Merchant Accounts originally covered by the TS ISO Agreement and the PCS ISO Agreement, the [***] day (or first Business Day thereafter if such day is not a Business Day) of each calendar month during the term of this Agreement; and (ii) for all other Merchant Accounts on the FDMS "Omaha" platform covered by this Agreement, the first Business Day after the day the funds are available and in Bank's settlement account, but in no event later than [***] [***] days after the end of each month.
perturbation.changed_text: "Payment Date" means: (i) for Merchant Accounts originally covered by the TS ISO Agreement and the PCS ISO Agreement, the [***] day (or first Business Day thereafter if such day is not a Business Day) of each calendar month during the term of this Agreement; and (ii) for all other Merchant Accounts on the FDMS "Omaha" platform covered by this Agreement, the first Business Day after the day the funds are available and in Bank's settlement account, and no later than the next business day after funds are available.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by modifying the deadline for payment for Merchant Accounts on the FDMS "Omaha" platform. Originally, payment was due no later than a specified number of days after the end of each month. Now, payment is due no later than the next business day after the funds are available in Bank's settlement account. This contradicts the initial clause for other Merchant Accounts, creating uncertainty about the payment schedule and enforcement. The original agreement had a time frame, while the modified payment is dependent on when the funds are available.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Payment Date" means: (i) for Merchant Accounts originally covered by the TS ISO Agreement and the PCS ISO Agreement, the [***] day (or first Business Day thereafter if such day is not a Business Day) of each calendar month during the term of this Agreement; and (ii) for all other Merchant Accounts on the FDMS "Omaha" platform covered by this Agreement, the first Business Day after the day the funds are available and in Bank's settlement account, and no later than the next business day after funds are available.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the 'Payment Date' definition for FDMS 'Omaha' Merchant Accounts, replacing a defined timeframe ( '[***] [***] days after the end of each month') with a variable deadline ('the next business day after funds are available'). This creates an inconsistency, as the original clause provided a fixed deadline, while the modified version ties the deadline to the availability of funds, contradicting the contradicted text's specification of a fixed timeframe and introducing ambiguity regarding the payment schedule. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Applications. ISO shall solicit Applications from Eligible Merchants at ISO's sole expense and shall provide each Applicant with Application Materials. ISO shall collect completed and signed Application Materials and Merchant Processing Agreements from Applicants and shall forward them to SERVICERS within [***] [***] Business Days after the merchant is boarded (which may be accessed by SERVICERS online or forwarded to SERVICERS via ISO's automated application system or to a facsimile number designated by SERVICERS, provided such complies with the Rules and applicable laws, rules and regulations) or to any other place(s) as SERVICERS may designate for processing and document storage. ISO shall retain copies of all Application Materials, Merchant Processing Agreements and documents forwarded to SERVICERS. ISO shall provide SERVICERS with access to ISO's automated application system and ISO's online Merchant Account system (BAMS, or any such successor or replacement system) at no cost to SERVICERS.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Applications. ISO shall solicit Applications from Eligible Merchants at ISO's sole expense and shall provide each Applicant with Application Materials. ISO shall collect completed and signed Application Materials and Merchant Processing Agreements from Applicants and shall forward them to SERVICERS within 90 Business Days after the merchant is boarded (which may be accessed by SERVICERS online or forwarded to SERVICERS via ISO's automated application system or to a facsimile number designated by SERVICERS, provided such complies with the Rules and applicable laws, rules and regulations) or to any other place(s) as SERVICERS may designate for processing and document storage. ISO shall retain copies of all Application Materials, Merchant Processing Agreements and documents forwarded to SERVICERS. ISO shall provide SERVICERS with access to ISO's automated application system and ISO's online Merchant Account system (BAMS, or any such successor or replacement system) at no cost to SERVICERS.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes the timeframe for forwarding application materials from a previously defined period to a fixed 90 business days. This creates an in-text contradiction if, elsewhere in the contract (e.g., service level agreements, specific clauses dealing with high-risk merchants), shorter timeframes are specified or implied for certain actions based on receiving these application materials. It introduces uncertainty because the general term of 90 days conflicts with more specific obligations that rely on a faster turnaround.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For any high risk Merchant who in good faith ISO anticipates will have annual Visa and MasterCard transaction volume equal to or greater than $[***] and any low risk Merchant who in good faith ISO anticipates will have annual Visa and MasterCard transaction volume equal to or greater than $[***], ISO shall advise SERVICERS and permit SERVICERS to conduct their own credit review prior to boarding the account. Upon receipt of a complete underwriting package from ISO, SERVICERS will respond to ISO within [***] [***] Business Days as to SERVICERS' acceptance or rejection of such Merchant. SERVICERS' participation in any credit review will not in any way relieve ISO from its responsibility for credit and fraud losses which may result from a Merchant's transaction processing. SERVICERS may at any time require that a Merchant relationship be terminated by ISO and Bank may cease the provision of services to any such Merchant pursuant to the terms of the Merchant Processing Agreement. SERVICERS shall have the right and authority to accept or reject any Application. ISO shall obtain prospective Merchants' authorization for ISO and/or SERVICERS to obtain all reports (including personal and business credit reports) and other information necessary in connection with the Application. ISO shall have the right, subject to and pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Merchant Processing Policy, to cause any Eligible Merchant with transaction card volume processing limits up to but not more than $[***] per month, that has been approved by ISO, to be activated immediately in SERVICES' systems. SERVICERS agree to provide ISO with a final approval decision (i.e., acceptance, rejection or required modification of the submitted Application) on submitted and complete Merchant Applications for Eligible Merchants that are not classified as "high risk" on the High Risk Merchant List attached hereto as Exhibit F and which seek approval for transaction card volume processing limits in excess of $[***] per month but not more than $[***] per month, within [***] [***] Business Days after SERVICERS' receipt of such completed Merchant Application and documentation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies a 90-day timeframe for forwarding application materials. The contradicted text, however, establishes shorter timeframes ([***] [***] Business Days) for SERVICERS to respond to ISO regarding high-risk merchants after receiving a complete underwriting package. Since the underwriting package likely contains the application materials, the 90-day period directly conflicts with the shorter response times required elsewhere, thus creating a clear in-text contradiction. The changed text impairs the obligations regarding processing times as stated within the contradicted text. Therefore, it adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: From time to time, ISO may request SERVICERS to disburse monies held in a Merchant Reserve Account to ISO or an Approved Merchant as set forth in the preceding sentence, and SERVICERS shall consider such requests in good faith. Bank may draw against any Merchant Reserve Account to cover any Losses or credit and fraud losses incurred by SERVICERS with respect to an Approved Merchant without the consent of ISO or the applicable Approved Merchant.
perturbation.changed_text: From time to time, ISO may request SERVICERS to disburse monies held in a Merchant Reserve Account to ISO or an Approved Merchant as set forth in the preceding sentence, and SERVICERS shall consider such requests in good faith. Bank may draw against any Merchant Reserve Account to cover any Losses or credit and fraud losses incurred by SERVICERS with respect to an Approved Merchant only after obtaining explicit consent from ISO or the applicable Approved Merchant.
perturbation.explanation: This creates a contradiction around the authority to draw from the Merchant Reserve Account. The original text allows Bank to draw against the account without consent, while the modified text requires explicit consent. This impacts risk allocation and financial control, leading to ambiguity in the agreement. It creates an in-text contradiction because previously, it was understood Servicers can draw against the reserve, but now it states they can't without ISO's agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: [***] ISO will monitor daily Merchant account activity and if in ISO's reasonable judgment certain Approved Merchants and/or certain transactions are possibly fraudulent or otherwise not in *** Omitted pursuant to a confidential treatment request. The confidential portion has been filed separately with the SEC. Page 13 compliance with the Rules or in violation of the Merchant Processing Agreement, ISO will instruct SERVICERS to change the Merchant bank account information to cause funds otherwise due such Merchant to be diverted and deposited into the Merchant Reserve Account for such Merchant. ISO will thereafter promptly investigate each such suspicious incident and, upon request, promptly notify SERVICERS of the result of any such investigation. If in any case ISO's investigation determines that no violation occurred, ISO will promptly request SERVICERS to release any funds diverted to the Merchant Reserve Account to the Merchant's deposit account. Once monies or other collateral are deposited in a Merchant Reserve Account, SERVICERS shall control the disbursements of such funds which are to be (a) paid to ISO or SERVICERS to cover losses incurred in connection with the applicable Approved Merchant or (b) returned to the applicable Approved Merchant upon a reduction in the potential risk to ISO and SERVICERS arising from the provision of services to such Merchant within two (2) Business Days after receipt of a written request by ISO. From time to time, ISO may request SERVICERS to disburse monies held in a Merchant Reserve Account to ISO or an Approved Merchant as set forth in the preceding sentence, and SERVICERS shall consider such requests in good faith. Bank may draw against any Merchant Reserve Account to cover any Losses or credit and fraud losses incurred by SERVICERS with respect to an Approved Merchant only after obtaining explicit consent from ISO or the applicable Approved Merchant.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Bank to draw against the Merchant Reserve Account without consent from ISO or the Merchant. The changed text now requires explicit consent from ISO or the Merchant before the Bank can draw against the account. This directly contradicts the prior allowance of drawing without consent, creating an inconsistency regarding the bank's authority over the account. The contradicted text includes both the prior, unmodified, text, and also includes the broader context of ISO's monitoring and instruction rights related to these accounts, increasing the potential for conflict.
